user_query:
I’m juggling my classes and our capstone team at UPR this week. Please help me consolidate the week’s schedule, prep meeting notes, and draft messages using the inputs in ./input. Use the fixed week window 2026-04-18 through 2026-04-25 (inclusive).

Requirements:
1) Create a merged weekly schedule at output/week_schedule.csv by combining records from input/events.csv and input/tasks.csv as follows:
   - Filter to records whose date falls within 2026-04-18 to 2026-04-25 inclusive (use due_date for tasks and date for events).
   - Add a column kind with value "task" for tasks and "event" for events.
   - Add a numeric score column set to priority for tasks and importance for events.
   - Sort all filtered records by score descending, then by date/time ascending.
   - Add a rank column (1..N) reflecting that sort order across the combined list.
   - Include at least these columns in the output, with values coming from the source files where applicable: kind, id, title_or_task (use title for events and task for tasks), course, date, time, location (empty for tasks), assigned_to (empty for events), score, rank.

2) Inspect the agenda files in input/agendas/ and identify the agenda for the capstone team meeting that week:
   - Find the team_meeting event in 2026-04-18..2026-04-25 from input/events.csv (there should be exactly one on 2026-04-22 at 16:00) and choose the agenda file in input/agendas whose date in the filename matches that meeting date.
   - Produce output/inspected/agenda_choice.txt listing all files found under input/agendas and clearly indicating which one you selected.

3) Prepare meeting notes for that capstone meeting at output/meeting_notes/notes_2026-04-22.md that include:
   - Title with the meeting name and date, the time and location pulled from the matching event, and a short “Purpose” line.
   - Agenda section populated from the selected agenda file’s bullet list under “Agenda:”.
   - Attendees section listing all team members (role == student) from input/team_roster.csv.
   - Action Items section with the top 3 Capstone tasks for the week by the rank computed in step 1 (if fewer than 3 Capstone tasks exist in the window, include all that exist). Each action item must include the assignee (from assigned_to), the task name, and the due date/time.

4) Draft messages using input/team_roster.csv and save them as plain text files:
   - For each student (role == student), create output/emails/team/<email>.txt with a Subject line “Capstone 2026-04-22 meeting notes & your action items” and a body that includes: the meeting date/time/location, the agenda summary (bullet list of agenda items), and any action item(s) from the notes assigned to that person (or a line stating “No new action items assigned” if none).
   - Create a single advisor summary at output/emails/advisor.txt addressed to the advisor (role == advisor) with a concise recap: meeting date/time/location, agenda highlights, and a bullet list of the Action Items (assignee, task, due date/time).

Use only the provided inputs. Do not modify files under ./input. Make sure all output paths match exactly so I can review the results. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
